hoses: Supply line from hydrant to Engine 181 intake
Relay Segment 1: 1000 ft of 5-inch LDH from Engine 181 discharge to Relay Engine intake
Relay Segment 2: 1000 ft of 5-inch LDH from Relay Engine discharge to Attack Engine intake
nozzles: Attack Engine deck gun with 1⅜-inch smooth bore tip
appliances: Deck gun mounted on Attack Engine
sceneElements.elevation: Level roadway operation, 0 PSI elevation correction

problems.title: Engine 181 Relay Discharge Pressure
problems.question: Engine 181 must deliver 500 GPM through 1000 ft of 5-inch LDH to the relay engine and maintain 20 PSI intake pressure at the relay engine. The LDH friction loss is 20 PSI and there is no elevation change. What discharge pressure should Engine 181 pump?
formulaBreakdown: Relay PDP = relay hose FL + elevation + desired intake pressure.
FL = C × (GPM / 100)^2 × hose length in hundreds.
Relay Segment 1 uses 5-inch LDH, so C = 0.08.
problems.instructorExplanation: Engine 181 must overcome friction loss in the relay line and still deliver the desired intake pressure to the relay engine. With 1000 ft of 5-inch LDH flowing 500 GPM, the friction loss is 20 PSI. Adding the 20 PSI desired intake pressure gives 40 PSI.
problems.explainMistake: Do not calculate only the relay hose friction loss. A relay engine must supply enough pressure to overcome friction loss and still leave the receiving engine with the required intake pressure.

problems.title: Relay Engine Discharge Pressure
problems.question: The relay engine must deliver 500 GPM through 1000 ft of 5-inch LDH to the attack engine and maintain 20 PSI intake pressure at the attack engine. The LDH friction loss is 20 PSI and there is no elevation change. What discharge pressure should the relay engine pump?
formulaBreakdown: Relay PDP = relay hose FL + elevation + desired intake pressure.
FL = C × (GPM / 100)^2 × hose length in hundreds.
Relay Segment 2 uses 5-inch LDH, so C = 0.08.
problems.instructorExplanation: The relay engine supplies the attack engine through the second relay segment. The second segment has the same hose length and flow as the first segment, so the required discharge pressure is also 40 PSI.
problems.explainMistake: Do not add the pressure from Engine 181 to this calculation. Each relay engine calculates its own discharge pressure based on the hose it is supplying and the desired intake pressure at the next engine.

problems.title: Attack Engine Pump Discharge Pressure
problems.question: The attack engine is supplying a deck gun master stream at 80 PSI nozzle pressure. The deck gun is elevated 20 ft above the pump; use elevation pressure = height × 0.5 PSI/ft. It is mounted on the attack engine, with no hose friction loss or appliance loss. What pump discharge pressure should the attack engine set?
formulaBreakdown: PP = FL + NP + elevation + appliance loss.
problems.instructorExplanation: The attack engine only calculates the pressure for the mounted master stream; relay-line losses are handled by relay engines. Add the local elevation to nozzle pressure.
problems.explainMistake: Do not add relay segment friction loss to the attack engine discharge pressure.

problems.title: Total Flow Requirement
problems.question: The relay operation is supplying one attack-engine deck gun with a 1⅜-inch smooth-bore tip operating at 80 PSI nozzle pressure. Use the smooth-bore flow formula: GPM = 29.7 × diameter² × √NP. What is the approximate total flow requirement for the relay operation?
formulaBreakdown: Smooth bore flow formula: GPM = 29.7 × d² × √NP.
problems.instructorExplanation: The relay flow requirement is based on the flowing master stream, not on the number of relay engines in the chain.
problems.explainMistake: Do not add 500 GPM for each relay segment. The same water moves through each relay engine.
